metadata.title: Flow 0: Data Acquisition — MiniVess download, external test datasets, DVC, OME-TIFF (#328 #139 #150 #176)

child-plan.critical-rules: - Rule #2: TDD MANDATORY — failing tests first, then implementation. - Rule #3: Library-First — use existing EBRAINS API, httpx for downloads, dvc Python API for versioning. - Rule #16: NO regex for parsing. Use json.loads(), pathlib.Path, str.split(). - Rule #17: ALL flows via Prefect in Docker. scripts/download_minivess.py is a UTILITY, not a run path. - Rule #18: /tmp FORBIDDEN for artifacts. Use pytest tmp_path for tests, volume-mounted /data in Docker. - Rule #22: .env.example is single source of truth. DATA_DIR, SPLITS_DIR already defined there. - Rule #23: Hydra-zen for experiment config — acquisition flow parameters go through Hydra. - MONAI Library-First: use monai.transforms for any NIfTI manipulation. - Academic scope: issue description should mention real-time acquisition literature (see Phase 0).

phase.name: Update GitHub issue #328 with academic context
phase.description: Update the GitHub issue #328 body with academic context for real-time acquisition systems. This is documentation-only — no code changes. Sets the stage for the paper Discussion section.

task.name: Update #328 issue body with acquisition literature
task.description:
Add a "Future Work / Discussion" section to issue #328 mentioning real-time adaptive acquisition systems as the natural evolution of this pipeline:
References to include: - Bhatt et al. (2013) nmeth.3041 — Apache Spark-based acquisition (Nature Methods) - Bhatt/pysight (2016) 10.1101/061507 — real-time multi-photon (bioRxiv) - Bhatt/STNeuroNet — github.com/soltanianzadeh/STNeuroNet - PBLab/python-pysight — github.com/PBLab/python-pysight - Nature Methods Real-time: doi.org/10.1016/j.neuron.2015.11.037 - nmeth.3217 — Nature Methods - science.1260922 — Science - pnas.1514209112 — PNAS real-time - Draelos et al. (2025) s41467-025-64856-3 — Improving adaptive neuroscience platform (file: sci-llm-writer/biblio/biblio-vascular/vascular-tmp/draelos-2025-improv-adaptive-neuroscience-platform.md) - Inverse design / hardware: dx.doi.org/10.1364/BOE.6.003113 (custom 2-PM system) - opg.optica.org/oe/fulltext.cfm?uri=oe-34-5-8961 (custom 2-PM additions)
Also note that the MiniVess dataset itself came from a 2PM system, and that the acquisition_flow.py (Flow 0) simulates this pipeline in a reproducible way for teams without access to live microscopy infrastructure.

phase.name: Prefect acquisition_flow wrapping existing download logic
phase.description: The existing scripts/download_minivess.py already handles EBRAINS download + ZIP extraction. This phase wraps it as a proper Prefect acquisition_flow with tasks, DVC versioning, and manual fallback instructions. The flow is the first in the 9-flow pipeline.

task.name: RED: Write failing tests for acquisition flow
GREEN: Implement run_acquisition_flow
task.files-to-modify: scripts/download_minivess.py (thin wrapper calling downloader.py) deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml (add acquisition service)

task.name: OME-TIFF to NIfTI conversion task

phase.name: External test dataset acquisition (#139 #150 #176)
phase.description: Implement download + DVC tracking for the 3 external test datasets. Closes #139 (acquisition), #150 (license verification), #176 (download helpers). These datasets are TEST-ONLY — never used in training folds.

task.name: RED: Tests for external dataset download
task.description:
Create tests/unit/data/test_external_dataset_download.py:
class TestExternalDatasetRegistry: - test_all_three_datasets_registered() — EXTERNAL_DATASETS has deepvess, tubenet_2pm, vesselnn - test_license_fields_present() — each entry has license + license_verified bool - test_tubenet_license_verified() — tubenet_2pm.license_verified is True (CC-BY-4.0) - test_vesselnn_license_verified() — vesselnn.license_verified is True (MIT) - test_deepvess_license_unverified() — deepvess.license_verified is False (eCommons-educational) - test_each_dataset_has_url() — each entry has source_url non-empty
class TestExternalDatasetDownloader: - test_download_creates_output_dir(tmp_path) — output_dir created even if download skipped - test_skip_if_already_present(tmp_path) — idempotent: skip if target dir non-empty - test_download_raises_on_unknown_dataset() — ValueError for unknown dataset name - test_manual_instructions_returned_on_failure() — returns dict with download_url key
class TestDVCConfigForExternalDatasets: - test_dvc_config_exists_for_each_dataset() — DVC_CONFIGS dict has 3 entries - test_dvc_path_format() — path follows "data/external/{name}.dvc" pattern

task.name: GREEN: Extend external_datasets.py with download helpers
task.description:
Extend src/minivess/data/external_datasets.py:
1. Add license_verified: bool field to ExternalDatasetConfig 2. Update DeepVess: license="eCommons-educational", license_verified=False 3. Update TubeNet 2PM: license="CC-BY-4.0", license_verified=True 4. Update VesselNN: license="MIT", license_verified=True 5. Implement download_external_dataset(name, output_dir) function: - Checks if target dir already populated (idempotent) - For tubenet_2pm: downloads from UCL Figshare API (httpx) - For vesselnn: clones GitHub repo or downloads release tarball - For deepvess: downloads from Cornell eCommons (httpx) — warn on unverified license - On failure: returns dict with manual instructions (never raises silently) 6. Add DatasetDVCConfig for per-dataset DVC tracking config 7. Add discover_external_test_pairs(name, data_dir) function

task.name: Wire external datasets into acquisition_flow
task.description: Add download_external_datasets_task to acquisition_flow.py. Flow downloads external datasets concurrently (Prefect task concurrency). Results logged to MLflow with eval_external_datasets_available tag. If external dataset download fails: log warning, continue (best-effort).

phase.name: DVC versioning integration
phase.description: Implement automatic DVC tagging after successful download. Each acquisition flow run produces a DVC commit + version tag.

task.name: DVC auto-commit in acquisition flow
task.description:
After successful download of MiniVess + external datasets: 1. `dvc add data/raw/minivess/` (update minivess.dvc) 2. `dvc add data/external/{name}/` for each external dataset 3. `git add data/*.dvc data/external/*.dvc` 4. Create version tag: "minivess/v{YYYY-MM-DD}" or increment semver 5. Log DVC commit hash + version tag to MLflow run
Expose as @task(name="dvc-commit-datasets") in acquisition_flow.py. Uses subprocess.run for DVC CLI commands (Python DVC API is unstable). Error handling: if dvc not installed, log warning + continue.

phase.name: Docker service + Hydra config
phase.description: Add acquisition Prefect flow service to docker-compose.flows.yml. Add configs/experiment/acquisition.yaml as Hydra-zen experiment config.

task.name: Docker service for acquisition flow
task.description:
Add to deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml:
acquisition: image: ${MINIVESS_BASE_IMAGE:-minivess-base:latest} environment: <<: *x-common-env FLOW_NAME: acquisition volumes: - ${DATA_DIR:-./data}:/data - ${MLRUNS_DIR:-./mlruns}:/mlruns command: ["python", "-m", "minivess.orchestration.flows.acquisition_flow"]
Note: acquisition flow does NOT need GPU (download + OME-TIFF conversion is CPU-only).

task.name: Hydra-zen experiment config for acquisition
task.description:
Create configs/experiment/acquisition.yaml:
defaults: - base - _self_
acquisition: force_download: false external_datasets: - deepvess - tubenet_2pm - vesselnn dvc_auto_commit: true ometiff_input_dir: null # set to path when converting real microscope data ometiff_voxel_size_um: [1.0, 1.0, 1.0]
mlflow_experiment: minivess_acquisition

task.name: Run full test suite + pre-commit
task.steps: uv run pytest tests/integration/orchestration/test_acquisition_flow_smoke.py -q uv run pytest tests/unit/data/test_external_dataset_download.py -q uv run pre-commit run --all-files uv run mypy src/minivess/orchestration/flows/acquisition_flow.py uv run mypy src/minivess/data/downloader.py uv run mypy src/minivess/data/external_datasets.py

task.name: Commit and push PR
task.steps: git checkout -b feat/acquisition-flow git add src/minivess/orchestration/flows/acquisition_flow.py git add src/minivess/data/downloader.py src/minivess/data/external_datasets.py git add src/minivess/data/converters.py src/minivess/data/CLAUDE.md git add configs/experiment/acquisition.yaml configs/dvc/remotes.yaml git add deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml git add tests/integration/orchestration/test_acquisition_flow_smoke.py git add tests/unit/data/test_external_dataset_download.py git add docs/datasets/README.md git commit -m "feat(acquisition): Flow 0 — MiniVess + external test datasets + DVC (#328 #139 #150 #176)" git push -u origin feat/acquisition-flow gh pr create --title "feat(acquisition): Flow 0 — data acquisition + external datasets + DVC" \ --body "Closes #328, #139, #150, #176"
